steps.title: Spot the contrast structure
steps.content: The sentence pairs two directly opposing observations linked by yet: studies show austerity has negligible electoral consequences, but recent European governments suffered electorally due to austerity. This structure—presenting a collision between research findings and real-world events—is the underlined sentence's core function.

steps.title: Match the structure to the answer choices
steps.content: Choice B describes the sentence as identifying “a conflict between research findings and recent events,” which is exactly its structure. The text then attributes this conflict to what Huebscher calls “a complicating factor in the data used to generate those findings”—governments' strategic timing of austerity measures that obscured the true electoral risks in the historical data. The other three choices either trace the conflict to insufficient real-world data in the studies, imagine research disagreement, or fault researchers' analysis. That matches choice B — B is correct.
